uvicorn on port 8001.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(installer): auto-install memory daemon with Python venv

The memory daemon source was not included in the npm package and the
installer did not install it. Users had to manually pip install the
daemon, which nobody did. This meant the MCP server never started and
Claudia had no memory capabilities.

Changes:
- Bundle daemon source (claudia_memory/ + pyproject.toml) in npm package
- Add "Memory Daemon" step to installer: detects Python 3.10+, creates
  venv at ~/.claudia/daemon/venv/, pip installs claudia-memory, verifies
- Add ensureDaemonMcpConfig() to auto-configure .mcp.json with correct
  venv Python path so Claude Code starts the daemon automatically
- Strengthen Session Start Protocol with mandatory three-outcome
  disclosure (tool responds / tool errors / tool missing)
- Add Mandatory Disclosure section to memory-availability rule requiring
  upfront honesty when daemon is unavailable
- Update .mcp.json.example to reflect automatic setup

Daemon setup is non-blocking: if Python is missing or pip fails, the
installer warns but continues. All other steps remain unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(fork): force print mode (-p) when resumeSessionAt is provided

--resume-session-at only works in -p (print) mode. When forking
from a specific assistant turn, persistentSession must be false so
Claude receives the -p flag, replays up to that turn, exits after
one response, then subsequent messages use the normal persistent path.

commit.message:
fix(#458): populate type_kind in CourseSpeciality during ingestion (#509)

* fix(#458): populate type_kind in CourseSpeciality during ingestion

Two bugs caused the `type_kind` column in `CourseSpeciality` to always
be empty:

1. `SpecialtyExtractor._map_education_level_from_type()` misinterpreted
   course-type strings ("Обов'язкова", "Вільного вибору",
   "Професійно-орієнтована") as education level indicators, defaulting
   them all to BACHELOR. These are actually CourseTypeKind values.

2. `injector.py` used `course.specialities.add(speciality)` on a custom
   M2M through model (`CourseSpeciality`). Django `.add()` cannot set
   extra fields on through models — `type_kind` was always left empty.

Changes:

**deduplicated.py**
- Add `CourseTypeKind` enum (COMPULSORY, ELECTIVE, PROF_ORIENTED)
- Add `type_kind: CourseTypeKind | None` field to `DeduplicatedSpeciality`

**extractors.py**
- Replace `_map_education_level_from_type()` with two methods:
  - `_extract_education_level()` — derives BACHELOR/MASTER from
    `data.education_level` (the course-level field)
  - `_map_course_type_kind()` — maps UA type strings to `CourseTypeKind`
- Add `_TYPE_KIND_MAP` class variable with backtick-normalized lookups

**injector.py**
- Replace `course.specialities.add(speciality)` with
  `CourseSpeciality.objects.update_or_create()` to properly set
  `type_kind` on the through model

**Tests**
- Add parametrized `test_specialty_extractor_type_kind_mapping` (7 cases)
- Add `test_specialty_extractor_education_level_from_course`
- Add `test_specialty_extractor_unknown_type_returns_none_type_kind`
- Update `test_injector_processes_specialities` to verify
  `update_or_create` with correct `type_kind` values

Scraped data distribution:
  Обов`язкова: 14,039 → COMPULSORY
  Професійно-орієнтована: 12,269 → PROF_ORIENTED
  Вільного вибору: 6,531 → ELECTIVE

Before: type_kind='' on all 8,435 CourseSpeciality rows (100%)

Note: to see fixes in production DB, a full reingestion is needed
(group_courses --force + insert_scraped). This will likely be done
after all three area:parser issues (#419, #458, #333) are resolved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(#458): remove redundant backtick variant from _TYPE_KIND_MAP

_map_course_type_kind() already normalizes backticks to apostrophes
via .replace("`", "'"), so the duplicate entry is unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(#458): skip CourseSpeciality upsert when type_kind is None

Passing an empty string to a choices field bypasses Django validation
silently. Instead, log a warning and skip creating the through-model
row when the specialty type cannot be mapped to a CourseTypeKind value.

Adds a test asserting update_or_create is not called for None type_kind.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: AI daily market briefing card on dashboard

- New /api/briefing route: fetches live SOL + Solana ecosystem prices from
  CoinGecko, then calls Groq llama-3.3-70b to generate 3-point briefing
- 1-hour server-side cache (Next.js revalidate = 3600) prevents API spam
- Graceful fallback to data-driven static insights when AI unavailable
- BriefingCard component: shows SOL price + 24h change, top gainer/loser badges,
  3 emoji-prefixed insight bullets, refresh button, "just now / Xm ago" timestamp
- Integrated in DashboardView below portfolio sparkline
